title: give me an overview of fibb's attendance, class spread, etc. note trends and do analysis on his activity in the guild

turns.userText: give me an overview of fibb's attendance, class spread, etc. note trends and do analysis on his activity in the guild
turns.agentText:
I'll resolve who "fibb" is first, then pull their attendance and stats.

Fibb = Phil (accounts Fibbs.1623 and Fibbs.1549). Let me pull his attendance and combat stats.

He runs on Fibbs.1623 (42 runs); Fibbs.1549 has no logged runs. Let me get his profession time breakdown and attendance detail.

Let me pull his month-by-month activity to surface trends.

Here's the picture on **Fibb (Phil — `Fibbs.1623`)**. His second account `Fibbs.1549` has zero logged runs, so everything below is the main. Data spans the guild's 66 logged runs, Feb 10 → Jun 16, 2026; he appears in **42** of them.

## Attendance

Through May, Fibb was a **rock-solid regular** — 57–77% of every raid night, hovering around 10 runs a month:

| Month | Attended / Logged | Rate | Combat hrs | Primary class |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Feb | 11 / 16 | 69% | 8.2 | Scourge |
| Mar | 8 / 14 | 57% | 6.0 | Harbinger |
| Apr | 10 / 13 | 77% | 7.8 | Firebrand |
| May | 11 / 16 | 69% | 9.2 | Firebrand |
| Jun (to 16th) | **2 / 7** | **29%** | 1.5 | Luminary |

{{figure}}

The headline trend is the **June drop-off**: from a steady ~70% attendance to 2 of 7 nights, and last seen **June 14**. One month is too short to call it a pattern — could be travel, a break, or real life — but after four consistent months it's the one thing worth a check-in. Everything before June points to a dependable, high-commitment raider (32.9 combat hours total, 13th-most combat time in the whole roster).

## Class spread

Fibb is one of the guild's **most flexible players** — he's logged time on 15 specs: Scourge, Harbinger, Firebrand, Luminary, Druid, Tempest, Reaper, Specter, Troubadour, Virtuoso, Amalgam, Ritualist, Untamed, Dragonhunter, and base Guardian.

But the month-by-month primary shows a clear **migration into dedicated frontline support**:

- **Feb–Mar:** backline pressure/utility (Scourge → Harbinger)
- **Apr–May:** settled onto **Firebrand** — core stability/aegis/quickness
- **Jun:** shifted to **Luminary** (the other meta stability/resistance guardian support)

So he's evolved from a flex-DPS/utility filler into a **stability anchor** — exactly the role you most want a reliable body in.

## Combat profile (42 runs)

His stat line confirms the support read — these are provider numbers, not DPS numbers:

- **Strips:** 19,518 (heavy boon-rip)
- **Cleanses:** 16,675
- **Healing:** 50.9M
- **DPS:** 1,389 (low, as expected for a stability bot)
- **Deaths:** 323 (~7.7/run — on the high side, typical of a frontline support eating the spike)

## Bottom line

A **high-value, high-flexibility support regular** who matured into a Firebrand/Luminary stability anchor through the spring. The only flag is the sharp **June attendance dip** — worth a friendly check-in to see whether it's a temporary break or something that needs roster planning around. Want me to pull his last couple of June runs to see how he performed, or draft a check-in DM?

One data note: AxiBridge's published whole-history rollup still shows him at 25 runs/Firebrand-primary — that table is stale. The 42-run figure above is the live parse of all 66 reports (no runs skipped), and the monthly counts sum to it, so I'm trusting the live numbers.

rows.professions: Tempest, Luminary, Dragonhunter, Reaper, Specter, Harbinger, Troubadour, Virtuoso, Firebrand, Amalgam, Ritualist, Scourge, Guardian, Untamed, Druid

data.title: Fibb — runs attended vs. raids logged, by month
